Title: New far-right mayors' first steps target Europe, culture and unions
Subhead: Le Monde examined the first decisions made by newly-elected far-right mayors. While they have not yet kept their tax promises, almost all of them have increased their own allowances, hired police officers and targeted local figures who are politically hostile to them.

Title: Europe, epicenter of global warming, faces its own contradictions
Trump warns Middle East truce on 'massive life support,' as Iran says ready for any aggression
Sandra Laugier, philosopher: 'In France as in the US, never has freedom of expression been so often invoked by its enemies'
Subhead: The recent crisis that erupted at Grasset is symptomatic of a new wave of brutality in the public sphere, argues academic Sandra Laugier for Le Monde. In her view, popular culture is far more resilient to this than certain intellectual circles that believed themselves protected from the predatory tendencies of the far right.

Title: Suspicious transactions on oil markets: 'On Wall Street, this sense of impunity is clearly widespread'
Subhead: Insider trading is now commonplace on the New York Stock Exchange, where profits linked to oil sales connected to the situation in Iran are estimated to have reached several hundred million dollars, reports Le Monde's New York correspondent Nicolas Chapuis.

Title: Gaming clubs officially set up shop in Paris
Subhead: In early 2026, seven gaming establishments, where slot machines are prohibited, were made permanent after an eight-year trial period. The massive Partouche Casino Club, which is not a traditional casino, will have its grand opening on Tuesday.

Title: Cannes 2026: Film influencers, the new stars of the red carpet
Subhead: They are often young, sometimes with hundreds of thousands of followers on social media, and have completely reshaped the landscape of film promotion in the media. Filmmakers are increasingly relying on the influential power of these new ambassadors of the silver screen.

Subhead: One month before the kickoff of the 2026 World Cup, Le Monde correspondents share a glimpse of their country's relationship with football.

Title: Paris's La Villette reinvents itself as an urban climate laboratory
Subhead: Forty years after its inauguration by former French president François Mitterrand, the green lung of northeast Paris is transforming into a vibrant haven where animals, plants and children coexist.

Title: Women are featured on Cannes's poster, but still sidelined at the festival
Subhead: The Cannes Film Festival's choice of a still from 'Thelma & Louise' for its official poster has drawn criticism from the feminist collective 50/50, which points to the persistently low number of female directors in the official selection.
